results.identified_issue: There's no account system, so a 'user' is a local label with an active-user picker; answers get an optional userId (nil for seed/old data). The one-per-side rule is enforced in SubmitAnswerView after the AI classifies the side (claimedSide is nil at submit), using AnswerStore.hasAnswered(side:for:by:). Enforcing per-side after analysis is the only correct point since the side isn't known before then; a rejected alternative was a hard cap of two per question, but per-side is stricter and matches the request ('once for each side'). The feed intentionally still doesn't surface the author. Not verified by a device build (vigil builds); SourceKit showed transient whole-module 'cannot find type' noise after xcodegen regeneration, not real errors.
results.summary: Added a Users tab, attributed every answer to a user, and capped each user at one answer per side of a question.
results.detailed_description: Built the three things you asked for. First, there's a new Users tab (third tab, person.2 icon): a roster where you add, rename, delete, and switch the active user, each row showing how many answers carry that person's name. Second, every answer is now stamped with a userId — done quietly, the way you wanted: the Discover feed still never names the author, the association just lives in the data and on the Users tab. The field is optional so the seeded and previously-saved answers still load fine (they read as unattributed). Third, the two-per-question limit: a single user can post at most one answer per side, so at most two per question. Because the app decides which side an answer lands on with AI after you submit, I enforce the limit right after that classification and before saving — if the active user already argued that side, the submission is refused with a message telling you to switch users or edit the existing answer. The composer now also shows an 'Answering as' row so you know who you're posting as. To try it: answer a question, then answer the same question the same way again as the same user — the second is blocked; switch users in the Users tab and it goes through. I regenerated the Xcode project to pick up the new files. I did not run a device build (vigil handles that), so this is verified by reading, not by running.

description: Okay, let's add a user tab as well, because what we want to do actually is associate each answer to a particular user, even though we don't necessarily want to indicate that. And then a single user should only be able to respond twice, once for each side of an argument.
